title: Spelling Bee Champion Makes History
story: Zaila Avant-garde buzzed with excitement as she spelled her way to victory. On July 8, 2021, this 14-year-old whiz kid from Harvey, Louisiana, became the Scripps National Spelling Bee champion. Zaila's triumph was extra special because she was the first African American student to win the prestigious competition. Her winning word? 'Murraya,' a type of tropical tree. But Zaila isn't just a spelling ace - she's also a basketball prodigy with amazing dribbling skills!

title: Voyager 2's Cosmic Snapshot
story: Zooming through space, Voyager 2 made an exciting discovery on July 8, 1979. The plucky spacecraft snapped the first-ever photo of Adrastea, one of Jupiter's tiny moons. This cosmic paparazzi moment revealed a celestial body so small it had escaped notice until then. Adrastea, also known as Jupiter XIV, orbits close to the giant planet's dazzling rings. Voyager 2's groundbreaking image helped scientists learn more about Jupiter's complex family of moons.

activity_choices: Adrastea, one of Jupiter's tiny moons
activity_answer: Adrastea, one of Jupiter's tiny moons

title: Ancient Voyagers: Polynesians Meet Americans
story: Imagine sailing across vast oceans without modern tools! A groundbreaking study in 2020 revealed an amazing discovery. title: China's Basketball Pioneer Shoots for the Stars
story: Towering talent Wang Zhizhi was born in Beijing on July 8, 1977. This giant of the court would become China's first-ever NBA player! His groundbreaking journey opened doors for future Chinese basketball stars to shine on the world stage.

title: A Musical Journey from Beijing to Broadway
story: Imagine a young boy in Beijing dreaming of creating beautiful music. That boy was Zhou Long, born on July 8, 1953.
